sections.subtitle: Current Form
sections.text: Inter are in excellent form, winning 4 of their last 5 matches, including a dominant 3-0 victory over Fiorentina. Milan, on the other hand, remain unbeaten in their last 11 games, with 3 wins and 2 draws in their recent 5 matches, showing resilience with late goals and comebacks.

sections.subtitle: Head-to-Head Record
sections.text: Milan have had the upper hand in recent Derby della Madonnina clashes, with no losses in their last 5 encounters against Inter, including a convincing 3-0 win in their most recent matchup. Historically, matches between these two often see goals from both sides, with 6 of the last 7 games having both teams scoring.

sections.subtitle: Key Players to Watch
sections.text: For Inter, Hakan Çalhanoğlu (7.78 average Sofascore rating) and Federico Dimarco have been pivotal in recent games, while Lautaro Martínez remains a constant goal threat. Milan's attack is led by in-form players like Christian Pulišić (4 goals in last 5) and Rafael Leão, supported by Luka Modrić's creativity in midfield.

sections.subtitle: Tactical Insights
sections.text: Inter's dominance in possession (60.3% average) and ability to create big chances (47 this season, league-best) could pressure Milan's defense. However, Milan's counter-attacking prowess and ability to score early (first to score in 8 of their last 10 games) might exploit Inter's occasional defensive lapses.

sections.subtitle: Season Statistics
sections.text: Inter lead the league in goals scored (26) and corners per game (7.27), showcasing their attacking intent, though they've underperformed their expected goals (-5.03). Milan, with 17 goals scored and a solid defense (9 conceded), are dangerous on the break and have shown consistency in avoiding defeat.

sections.subtitle: Match Prediction
sections.text: Given Inter's strong home form and attacking output, combined with Sofascore's 53% home win probability, they edge as favorites. However, Milan's recent head-to-head dominance and unbeaten streak suggest a tight contest, likely with goals on both sides.

halftimeSections.subtitle: Half-time overview
halftimeSections.text: The first half of this intense Milan derby at the San Siro ended in a goalless draw, with both Inter and Milan failing to convert their opportunities into goals. Inter dominated possession and created more dangerous moments, while Milan struggled to make a significant impact in the attacking third. The 0-0 scoreline reflects a tightly contested match, but Inter appear to have the upper hand heading into the second half.

halftimeSections.subtitle: Inter's first-half performance
halftimeSections.text: Inter controlled much of the game, enjoying a higher share of possession and creating several promising chances. They managed to generate a big chance but failed to capitalize on it, missing out on taking the lead. Their entries into the final third and penalty area were notably frequent, showcasing their intent to push forward. Additionally, Inter hit the woodwork twice, underlining their attacking threat despite not finding the net. Defensively, they looked solid, winning a majority of duels and aerial battles.

halftimeSections.subtitle: Milan's first-half performance
halftimeSections.text: Milan, on the other hand, had a more conservative first half, struggling to create clear-cut opportunities. They failed to register a big chance and lacked the same level of penetration in the final third as their rivals. Their possession was lower, and they found it difficult to win duels, both on the ground and in the air. Defensively, they held firm to keep the score level, but a yellow card picked up indicates some frustration in their ranks.

halftimeSections.subtitle: Attack momentum analysis
halftimeSections.text: Inter started the game with a strong attacking intent, maintaining consistent pressure in the early stages with several spells of dominance. Their momentum occasionally dipped, allowing Milan to gain some footing around the middle of the half with a brief period of sustained attacks. However, Inter regained control later in the half, pushing forward with renewed vigor in multiple attacking waves. Milan had moments of promise but lacked the consistency to truly threaten Inter's defense for extended periods.

halftimeSections.subtitle: Key players to watch
halftimeSections.text: For Inter, Hakan Çalhanoğlu has been a standout performer in midfield, creating opportunities and dictating play with key passes. Lautaro Martínez also looked dangerous, getting into good positions and registering shots on target. On the Milan side, Alexis Saelemaekers showed some flair with his involvement in play, while goalkeeper Mike Maignan has been crucial in keeping the scoreline level with a strong presence between the posts.

halftimeSections.subtitle: Second-half expectations
halftimeSections.text: The second half is likely to see Inter continue their attacking approach, leveraging their dominance in possession and final-third presence to break the deadlock. Milan will need to be more assertive in attack if they hope to turn the tide, possibly relying on counter-attacks to exploit any gaps left by Inter's forward push. Both teams are expected to push harder for a goal, but Inter's superior first-half performance gives them a slight edge.
